description: Veloce SNES authoritative test suite. Enumerates the standard public SNES test-ROM landscape organized by hardware subsystem so the testkit scoring layer can compute a defensible per-subsystem completeness percentage. PRIMARY SOURCE is the higan/byuu 'snes-test-roms' mirror (gitlab.com/higan/snes-test-roms), which bundles: Blargg SPC700/DSP/timer ROMs (blargg-spc-6/), the full PeterLemon/krom 65816 CPU opcode suite (CPUADC..CPUTRN), PeterLemon SPC700 + SuperFX/GSU opcode suites, absindx SA-1 tests, and a deep timing collection (jonasquinn, KungFuFurby, undisbeliever, Sour, 93143, Motive, lidnariq). Every file path below is a REAL basename present in that mirror; the runner's ROM provider resolves each test by basename across the cloned tree, so layout drift between mirrors is tolerated and unresolved ROMs report SKIP (excluded from score) rather than a false FAIL. Detection: Blargg-family ROMs report PASS/FAIL via the $6000 memory protocol (BLARGG_STATUS / 'Status code: N' printed under DEBUG=1). PeterLemon/krom and most timing ROMs draw PASS/FAIL on screen -> screenshot-crc; their reference CRC32 hashes are 'to be measured' (marked known_fail until captured with --generate-refs on a verified-good build) so they do not inflate the headline score.

blargg.license: mixed; Blargg test code public domain, PeterLemon MIT-style, others per-author (see each subdir README)
blargg.notes: higan/byuu master mirror. Single clone provides the entire ROM set referenced by this config. Archive fallback: https://github.com/MiSTer-devel/SNES_MiSTer/files/4109309/blargg_testroms.zip

apu_spc700_blargg.name: Blargg SPC700 CPU + timing (memory protocol)
apu_spc700_blargg.description: Blargg's SPC700 instruction-set and timing ROMs from blargg-spc-6/. Report PASS/FAIL via the $6000 memory protocol. spc_smp = master SPC700 instruction test; spc_mem_access_times = exact RAM/IPL/register access cycle timing; spc_timer = T0/T1/T2 divider+counter behavior. spc_dsp6 is a documented 3-chip-console quirk.

tests.notes: Master SPC700 opcode/flag test. MEASURED: currently traps on unimplemented SPC700 opcode $79 (CMP (X),(Y)) -> never reaches $6000. Real accuracy gap.
SPC700 T0/T1 (8kHz) + T2 (64kHz) divider and counter-latch timing.
Exact SPC700 read/write access cycle counts for RAM, IPL ROM, DSP/timer registers.
KNOWN HW QUIRK: fails on real 3-chip SNES; passes only on higan/bsnes. Excluded from headline denominator.

apu_spc700_ops.name: PeterLemon SPC700 opcode suites (visual)
apu_spc700_ops.description: krom/PeterLemon per-opcode SPC700 tests (ADC/AND/DEC/EOR/INC/ORA/SBC). Draw PASS/FAIL on screen -> screenshot-crc. Complement the Blargg master test with focused per-op coverage. Reference hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: SPC700 ADC. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 SBC. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 AND. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 EOR. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 ORA. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 INC. reference_hash to be measured.
SPC700 DEC. reference_hash to be measured.

apu_dsp_spctest.name: gilyon/krom SPC700 + DSP automated test (visual)
apu_dsp_spctest.description: gilyon spctest.sfc is a comprehensive automated SPC700/DSP test that renders a results table. Visual screenshot-crc; reference hash to be measured.

tests.notes: Automated SPC700+DSP results table. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_arith.name: 65816 arithmetic opcodes (krom CPUADC/CPUSBC)
cpu_arith.description: 65816 ADC/SBC across 8/16-bit (M flag), decimal mode (D flag) and all addressing modes. PeterLemon CPU suites render a per-instruction PASS/FAIL table -> screenshot-crc (visual). Reference hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: ADC binary + decimal, 8/16-bit. MEASURED crc32 on current build = 4714001a (frame 60); reference (verified-good) to be confirmed.
SBC binary + decimal, 8/16-bit. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_logic.name: 65816 logic/shift/rotate/bit opcodes (krom AND/EOR/ORA/BIT/ASL/LSR/ROL/ROR)
cpu_logic.description: Logical, shift, rotate and bit-test opcodes across addressing modes and M-flag widths. Visual screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: AND. reference_hash to be measured.
EOR. reference_hash to be measured.
ORA. reference_hash to be measured.
BIT sets N/V from operand bits 7/6 (and Z from AND). reference_hash to be measured.
ASL. reference_hash to be measured.
LSR. reference_hash to be measured.
ROL through carry. reference_hash to be measured.
ROR through carry. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_incdec_cmp.name: 65816 inc/dec and compare opcodes (krom INC/DEC/CMP)
cpu_incdec_cmp.description: INC/DEC (memory + A + X/Y) and CMP flag results across M/X widths. Visual screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: INC/INX/INY. reference_hash to be measured.
DEC/DEX/DEY. reference_hash to be measured.
CMP/CPX/CPY flag results. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_loadstore_transfer.name: 65816 load/store/transfer/stack opcodes (krom LDR/STR/MOV/TRN/PHL/PSR)
cpu_loadstore_transfer.description: Loads (LDR), stores (STR), block-move MVN/MVP (MOV), register transfers (TRN: TAX/TXA/TCD/TCS/TXY/TYX) and stack/processor-status ops (PHL: PHA/PLA/PHX/PHB/PHD/PEA/PEI/PER; PSR: PHP/PLP/REP/SEP). Visual screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: LDA/LDX/LDY all modes. reference_hash to be measured.
STA/STX/STY/STZ all modes. reference_hash to be measured.
Block move MVN/MVP (banked, length-in-A semantics). reference_hash to be measured.
Register transfers + XBA. reference_hash to be measured.
Push/pull family + PEA/PEI/PER. reference_hash to be measured.
Processor-status: PHP/PLP/REP/SEP flag-width effects. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_branch_jump_misc.name: 65816 branch/jump/return + misc/mode opcodes (krom BRA/JMP/RET/MSC)
cpu_branch_jump_misc.description: Conditional branches + BRL (BRA), JMP/JML/JSR/JSL (JMP), RTS/RTL/RTI (RET), and misc/mode opcodes incl. XCE/CLC/SEC/CLD/SED/CLI/SEI/CLV/NOP/WDM/STP/WAI (MSC). Critical for emulation<->native transitions and flow control. Visual screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: All conditional branches + BRL. reference_hash to be measured.
JMP/JML/JSR/JSL. reference_hash to be measured.
RTS/RTL/RTI semantics. reference_hash to be measured.
Flag ops + XCE/REP/SEP mode switches + NOP/WDM/STP/WAI. reference_hash to be measured.

cpu_full_automated.name: gilyon/krom full 65816 automated CPU test (visual)
cpu_full_automated.description: gilyon cputest.sfc is a single automated 65816 test that renders a results table (covers opcodes + flags + math registers). One-shot regression of the whole CPU. Visual screenshot-crc; reference hash to be measured.

tests.notes: Whole-CPU automated results table incl. multiply/divide register math. reference_hash to be measured.

ppu_hello_smoke.name: PPU bring-up smoke tests (jonasquinn)
ppu_hello_smoke.description: Minimal PPU rendering ROMs: test_hello (text tilemap) and test_noise (full-screen pattern). Cheap visual regressions that catch gross BG/CGRAM/tilemap breakage. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: BG tilemap + CGRAM text render smoke. reference_hash to be measured.
Full-screen pattern render. reference_hash to be measured.

ppu_oam.name: PPU OAM / sprite tests (jonasquinn)
ppu_oam.description: OAM table layout, OBJ sizes/priority, and the high/low OAM table fold ($2102/$2104 write, $2138 read). test_oam exercises sprite placement and the secondary OAM table. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: OAM main+secondary table, OBJ size/priority. Relates to fixed $2138 OOB-read fold + range/time-over flags. reference_hash to be measured.

ppu_mode3_colorhalve.name: PPU Mode 3 + color-math half-tests (tukuyomi/jonasquinn)
ppu_mode3_colorhalve.description: demo_mode3 renders a Mode 3 (8bpp direct-color) scene; color_halve_proof asserts the color-math 'halve' rule (halve always in fixed-color mode; in sub-screen mode only when sub pixel != backdrop). screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: Mode 3 8bpp direct color. reference_hash to be measured.
Color-math halve rule proof ($2131). reference_hash to be measured.

ppu_bus_inidisp.name: PPU bus activity + INIDISP/forced-blank glitches (lidnariq/undisbeliever)
ppu_bus_inidisp.description: ppubusact probes PPU-bus open-bus/MDR behavior on $2134-$213F reads; the undisbeliever hdma-2100 set probes the INIDISP ($2100) forced-blank + brightness glitch when written mid-frame via HDMA. Edge-case rendering correctness. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: PPU-bus / PPU1-PPU2 MDR open-bus on $2134-$213F. reference_hash to be measured.
$2100 forced-blank/brightness mid-frame HDMA glitch. reference_hash to be measured.

timing_irq.name: H/V IRQ + NMI timing (jonasquinn/KungFuFurby/tukuyomi)
timing_irq.description: Programmable H-IRQ ($4207/$4208 HTIME), V-IRQ ($4209/$420A VTIME), NMITIMEN ($4200) enable bits, $4211 TIMEUP acknowledge, and NMI vs IRQ edges. The status-bar split (V-IRQ at VTIME=31) regression that was fixed lives in this class. Cycle-accurate where a single dot of skew changes the split line. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: Core H/V-IRQ trigger. reference_hash to be measured.
$4200 NMITIMEN enable/disable edge timing for IRQ. reference_hash to be measured.
$4209/$420A V-IRQ exact trigger line. Directly covers the status-bar split. reference_hash to be measured.
NMI assert/clear edge vs VBlank. reference_hash to be measured.

timing_dma.name: General DMA transfers + timing (jonasquinn/Sour)
timing_dma.description: General-purpose DMA: $420B MDMAEN, transfer modes 0-7 (to $2118/$2119 VRAM, $2122 CGRAM...), address increment/fixed, per-byte 8-master-cycle cost + DMA-start alignment, and the open-bus/MDR value left after a transfer (test_dmavalid / test_mdrhdma). test_dmatiming asserts exact cycle cost. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: DMA transfer modes + increment. reference_hash to be measured.
Exact DMA cycle cost + start alignment penalty. reference_hash to be measured.
DMA open-bus/MDR value + valid-byte behavior. reference_hash to be measured.
DMA-vs-IRQ interaction (IRQ fires during a DMA): the hard case games like 'Ings' depend on. reference_hash to be measured.

timing_hdma.name: HDMA (H-blank DMA) timing (jonasquinn/93143/Motive)
timing_hdma.description: HDMA channel setup ($420C HDMAEN), per-scanline transfers, indirect mode, repeat/continue line-counter semantics, the exact H-position HDMA fires, mid-frame HDMA enable/disable, and HDMA-to-VRAM during H-blank. Air Strike Patrol-class raster effects depend on this. Cycle-accurate. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: Core HDMA incl. indirect + line-counter repeat. reference_hash to be measured.
Exact H-position at which each channel's HDMA transfer occurs. reference_hash to be measured.
HDMA-vs-CPU sync / mid-scanline register effect ordering. reference_hash to be measured.
Mid-frame HDMA enable/disable ($420C latch). reference_hash to be measured.
H-blank DMA to VRAM (window where VRAM writes are legal). reference_hash to be measured.
H-blank emulation + mid-scanline split correctness. reference_hash to be measured.

mapper_superfx.name: SuperFX (GSU) opcode tests (krom GSUTest)
mapper_superfx.description: GSU/SuperFX instruction suite: ALU (ADC/ADD/SUB/SBC/AND/OR/XOR/NOT/BIC), shifts/rotates (ASR/LSR/ROL/ROR), multiply (FMULT/LMULT/UMULT/MULT), load/store/immediate (IBT/IWT/MOVE/MOVES), and pixel/plot helpers (MERGE/SWAP/HIB/LOB/SEX/CACHEINJECT). Enhancement-chip coverage -> mapper subsystem. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: GSU ADC. reference_hash to be measured.
GSU signed multiply. reference_hash to be measured.
GSU fractional multiply. reference_hash to be measured.
GSU long multiply. reference_hash to be measured.
GSU ROL. reference_hash to be measured.
GSU MERGE (plot helper). reference_hash to be measured.

mapper_sa1.name: SA-1 enhancement chip tests (absindx)
mapper_sa1.description: SA-1 coprocessor: RAM write-protection register behavior and the SA-1 version/identification code. Enhancement-chip coverage -> mapper subsystem. screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: SA-1 BW-RAM write-protect register. reference_hash to be measured.
SA-1 version/ID code register. reference_hash to be measured.

memory_speed_openbus.name: Memory access speed + open bus (VitorVilela7/Sour)
memory_speed_openbus.description: SnesSpeedTest measures FastROM/SlowROM ($420D MEMSEL) access speed and the 3.58/2.68 MHz region cycle costs; Sour op_timing_test_v2 asserts per-opcode cycle counts including the memory-speed-dependent variants. Covers the memory-timing axis (and indirectly open-bus where unmapped reads feed the MDR). screenshot-crc; hashes to be measured.

tests.notes: FastROM/SlowROM ($420D) + region access-speed cycle costs. reference_hash to be measured.
Per-opcode cycle counts incl. memory-speed-dependent variants and DMA/HDMA interaction. The single best 5A22 cycle-timing regression. reference_hash to be measured.

known_issues.spc700_opcode_79: MEASURED: apu.spc_smp (Blargg SPC700 master test) traps on unimplemented SPC700 opcode $79 (CMP (X),(Y)) at SPC $0A99 and spins, so it never reaches the $6000 result write. This is a real, currently-open SPC700 core gap surfaced by this suite. Other SPC700-dependent ROMs may hit the same wall.
known_issues.spc_dsp6: spc_dsp6 fails on real 3-chip SNES consoles; only passes on higan/bsnes. Marked expected=known_fail (excluded from headline denominator, reported in its own column).
known_issues.krom_visual_refs: All krom/PeterLemon and community visual ROMs report PASS/FAIL on screen, so they use screenshot-crc. Until a golden reference frame is captured on a verified-good build (--generate-refs), they are marked expected=known_fail and contribute 0 to the headline score (honest 'to be measured' state) rather than being faked as pass. Reference hashes are tied to the fixed headless output resolution + PNG encoder; regenerate if either changes.
known_issues.mode5_subscreen: Mode 5 pseudo-hires currently drops the sub-screen at the frontend (snes_plugin.cpp ~531); any 512px ppu test will diff until addressed. (No standalone Mode 5 ROM in this mirror; covered indirectly by op/hdma timing ROMs that set hires.)
known_issues.rom_path_healing: Test 'file' paths are real basenames from the higan mirror, but mirror layouts drift. The runner builds a basename index across the cloned tree and symlinks matches into place; unresolved ROMs report SKIP (excluded from score) rather than fail.
